How I combined Yahoo API automation,
     Monte Carlo simulation, and
     strategic tanking to win it all

title: ЁЯПЖ Championship Run
text:
Season Journey


  
    Weeks 1тАУ5
    Injury Chaos Begins
    Top draft picks sidelined early. Thanks to the strong roster dominating FG%, REB, AST, and BLK categories, I reached rank 2 early.
  

  
    Weeks 6тАУ18
    Multiple Injuries, Holding Position
    Roster never fully healthy for 18 straight weeks. Utilized strategic waiver wire moves on category-specific strengths of REB, AST, and low TOs to maintain standings at rank 9.
  

  
    Week 19 тАФ Turning Point
    ЁЯФС Player come back + KD + Raynaud Trade
    4 players come back from injury after All-star break, and acquired Kevin Durant and Maxime Raynaud at low cost thanks to season ending sale. Projection model flagged an immediate boost across 3PT, PTS, STL.
  

  
    Weeks 19тАУ21
    Roster at Full Strength
    Joki─З, Durant, and Paul George all healthy simultaneously for the first time. Weekly win probabilities climbed above 85%.
  

  
    Week 22 тАФ The Tank
    ЁЯО▓ Strategic Seed 8 Decision
    Identified that Joki─З and Clingan had only 2 games in Week 23 due to NBA scheduling. Deliberately lost Week 21 to drop to Seed 8, avoiding a top-seed semi-final during the weakest week.
  

  
    Weeks 23тАУ24
    ЁЯПЖ Playoff Dominance
    Semi-finals won against a mid-seed opponent despite Joki─З's limited games. Finals entered with full roster and 91.7% simulated win probability.

title: ЁЯПЖ Championship Run
text:
The Technical Stack

  
    ЁЯдЦ
    Yahoo API Automation
    Daily automated pulls of roster stats, injury flags, and matchup data
       via OAuth. Output pushed directly to Excel reports each morning.
  
  
    ЁЯО▓
    Monte Carlo Simulation
    100,000 simulations per matchup using Poisson distributions per player
       per game. Generated category-level and overall win probabilities.
  
  
    ЁЯУК
    Excel Matchup Board
    Auto-generated weekly Excel boards showing projected vs. actual stats,
       games remaining, injury status, and category breakdowns.
  
  
    ЁЯПе
    Injury Tracking
    ESPN injury feed integrated with roster data. Automated GTD/OUT flags
       with season-average fallbacks to keep projections realistic.
  
  
    тЪЧя╕П
    Last-30 Baseline Stats
    Rolling 30-day averages as projection baseline, with season-stat
       fallback for players returning from injury.
  
  
    ЁЯМ▓
    Games Remaining Model
    NBA schedule scraper estimated remaining games per team each week,
       driving volume-adjusted projections that raw averages miss.

title: ЁЯПЖ Championship Run
text:
The Strategic Tank
My two best players had a critical NBA scheduling problem in Week 23:

Nikola Joki─З тАФ elite across all 7 categories, but only 2 games in Week 23
Donovan Clingan тАФ elite REB + BLK, but only 2 games in Week 23

This made Week 23 my weakest week of the playoffs regardless of seeding. The key question was: which opponent do I face in that weak week?


Code
import matplotlib.pyplot as plt
import matplotlib.patches as mpatches
import matplotlib.gridspec as gridspec

fig = plt.figure(figsize=(13, 6.5))
gs = gridspec.GridSpec(2, 1, hspace=0.7)

xs = [0.3, 4.2, 8.1]
week_labels = ['Week 22\n(Quarter-finals)', 'Week 23\n(Semi-finals)', 'Week 24\n(Finals)']
BOX_W, BOX_H = 3.5, 0.8

def draw_row(ax, title, title_color, boxes):
    ax.set_xlim(0, 12.5)
    ax.set_ylim(-0.05, 1.6)
    ax.axis('off')
    ax.text(0.2, 1.52, title, color=title_color,
            fontsize=10.5, fontweight='bold', va='top')

    for i, (label, sub, color) in enumerate(boxes):
        x = xs[i]
        rect = mpatches.FancyBboxPatch(
            (x, 0.45), BOX_W, BOX_H,
            boxstyle="round,pad=0.1",
            linewidth=1.3, edgecolor='#bbbbbb',
            facecolor=color
        )
        ax.add_patch(rect)
        ax.text(x + BOX_W/2,
                0.45 + BOX_H/2 + (0.1 if sub else 0),
                label, ha='center', va='center',
                color='white', fontsize=9, fontweight='bold')
        if sub:
            ax.text(x + BOX_W/2,
                    0.45 + BOX_H/2 - 0.18,
                    sub, ha='center', va='center',
                    color='#eeeeee', fontsize=7.8)
        ax.text(x + BOX_W/2, 0.38, week_labels[i],
                ha='center', va='top',
                color='#666666', fontsize=7.5)
        if i < len(boxes) - 1:
            ax.annotate('',
                xy=(xs[i+1] - 0.05, 0.45 + BOX_H/2),
                xytext=(x + BOX_W + 0.05, 0.45 + BOX_H/2),
                arrowprops=dict(arrowstyle='->', color='#888888', lw=1.5))

# PATH A: Seed 3-6
ax1 = fig.add_subplot(gs[0])
draw_row(ax1,
    title='PATH A тАФ Seed 3 to 6  (rejected)',
    title_color='#c62828',
    boxes=[
        ('QF: vs Seed 7/8',    'тЬЕ Joki─З 4G тАФ Win easily',       '#2e7d32'),
        ('SF: vs Seed 1 or 2', 'тЭМ Joki─З only 2G тАФ Cannot win',  '#c62828'),
        ('Finals: if reached', 'тЪая╕П Never gets here',             '#9e9e9e'),
    ]
)
ax1.text(12.3, 0.85, '~8%\nchamp\nprob',
         ha='right', va='center', fontsize=8.5,
         color='#c62828', fontweight='bold')

# PATH B: Seed 8
ax2 = fig.add_subplot(gs[1])
draw_row(ax2,
    title='PATH B тАФ Seed 8  тЬУ chosen',
    title_color='#2e7d32',
    boxes=[
        ('QF: vs Seed 1',      'тЪая╕П Joki─З 4G тАФ 50/50',           '#e65100'),
        ('SF: vs Seed 4 or 5', 'тЬЕ Joki─З 2G тАФ Still winnable',   '#1565c0'),
        ('Finals: Week 24',    'тЬЕ Full roster тАФ 91.7% win prob', '#1b5e20'),
    ]
)
ax2.text(12.3, 0.85, '~16%\nchamp\nprob',
         ha='right', va='center', fontsize=8.5,
         color='#2e7d32', fontweight='bold')

fig.suptitle(
    "The Tank Decision: Joki─З's Week 23 Schedule Determined Everything",
    fontsize=11.5, fontweight='bold', y=1.01
)
plt.tight_layout()
plt.show()










Round-by-round probability breakdown








Round
Seed 3тАУ6 Path
Seed 8 Path (chosen)




Week 22 (QF)
тЬЕ ~85% тАФ vs easy Seed 7/8
тЪая╕П ~50% тАФ vs Seed 1


Week 23 (SF)
тЭМ ~15% тАФ Joki─З 2G vs top seed
тЬЕ ~65% тАФ Joki─З 2G vs mid seed


Week 24 (Finals)
тЪая╕П ~50% if reached
тЬЕ ~92% тАФ full roster


Championship probability
85% ├Ч 15% ├Ч 50% тЙИ 6%
50% ├Ч 65% ├Ч 92% тЙИ 30%




Tanking to Seed 8 roughly quintupled the championship probability.

title: ЁЯПЖ Championship Run
text:
Automation Pipeline


Core daily pipeline (run_manual.py)
def main():
    # 1. OAuth authentication with Yahoo API
    client = YahooClient(auth)

    # 2. Fetch current matchup context
    matchup = get_current_matchup_context(client, league_key, my_team_key)

    # 3. Pull roster data for both teams
    my_roster  = get_team_roster(client, matchup.my_team_key)
    opp_roster = get_team_roster(client, matchup.opponent_team_key)

    # 4. Last-30-day stats as baseline
    #    (falls back to season avg for returning injured players)
    baseline_stats = get_player_last_30d_stats(client, all_player_keys)

    # 5. Estimate remaining games per NBA team this week
    games_remaining = estimate_games_remaining_by_team(
        week_end=matchup.week_end,
        team_abbrs=all_team_abbrs
    )

    # 6. Build projections:
    #    actuals so far + remaining games ├Ч per-game avg
    my_proj  = build_player_projection_table(...)
    opp_proj = build_player_projection_table(...)

    # 7. Write Excel matchup board + injury tracking sheet
    write_excel_report(output_path, my_proj, opp_proj, ...)

title: ЁЯПЖ Championship Run
text:
Key Takeaways






TipWhat Made the Difference




Baseline quality тАФ Last-30-day stats with injury-aware fallbacks gave realistic projections, not season averages skewed by early-season performance.
Games remaining model тАФ Knowing a player has 4 games vs 2 games this week often matters more than per-game averages alone.
Simulation over gut feel тАФ Monte Carlo revealed 3PM/TO were already lost before the week started, so focus shifted to winning the other 5 categories.
The tank was counter-intuitive тАФ Conventional wisdom says chase the highest seed. The scheduling data showed the opposite was optimal.




Built with Python ┬╖ Yahoo Fantasy API ┬╖ Monte Carlo Simulation ┬╖ Quarto
